sections.title: Part 2
sections.passage: You will hear a talk about Oniton Hall and activities on the farm.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: Many past owners made changes to
Sir Edward Downes built Oniton Hall because he wanted

options.text: a place to display his wealth.
a place for artists and writers.

questions.question: Visitors can learn about the work of servants in the past from
What is new for children at Oniton Hall?

questionGroups.instructions: Which activity is offered at each of the following locations on the farm? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 15–20.

sections.title: Part 3
sections.passage: You will hear two students discussing their reviews of Romeo and Juliet.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO things do the students agree they need to include in their reviews of Romeo and Juliet?

questionGroups.instructions: Which opinion do the speakers give about each of the following aspects of The Emporium's production of Romeo and Juliet? Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–G, next to Questions 23–27.

options.text: They both expected this to be more traditional.
They both thought this was original.
They agree this created the right atmosphere.
They agree this was a major strength.
They were both disappointed by this.
They disagree about why this was an issue.
They disagree about how this could be improved.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: The students think the story of Romeo and Juliet is still relevant for young people today because

options.text: it illustrates how easily conflict can start.
it deals with problems that families experience.

questions.question: The students found watching Romeo and Juliet in another language
Why do the students think Shakespeare's plays have such international appeal?

options.text: They can be interpreted in many ways.

sections.title: Part 4
sections.passage: You will hear a lecture about the impact of digital technology on the Icelandic language.

questionGroups.title: The impact of digital technology on the Icelandic language

that is still growing
